title: Netherlands vs Japan Picks, Predictions & Betting Odds: World Cup 2026 Preview - Yahoo Sports
original_text:
Netherlands vs Japan Picks, Predictions & Betting Odds: World Cup 2026 Preview
Netherlands vs Japan | FIFA World Cup 2026 Group F | Sunday, June 14, 2026 | Kickoff: 4:00 PM ET | AT&T Stadium, Dallas (Arlington) | TV: Fox Sports
Why This Game Matters
Group F opens with a fixture that will set the early tone for both sides’ tournament ambitions. Netherlands enter as the group’s most credentialed side, runners-up in 2010 and third-place finishers in 2014, while Japan arrive having beaten Germany, Spain, Brazil, and England in recent years and carrying a genuine belief they can finally push past the last-16 ceiling they have hit four times running. A win here gives either side a commanding early grip on the group; a defeat leaves recovery work to do against Sweden and Tunisia.
Advertisement
Best World Cup 2026 Offer Betfred 4.5 Welcome Bonus Bet and Get New Bettor Bonus of £50 in Free Bets BET NOW
Our Pick
Netherlands to win is the headline call at +112 with BetOnline, backed by a qualifying record of six wins and two draws without a defeat and a defensive unit that may be the strongest at the entire tournament. Japan are genuinely dangerous but the Dutch defensive structure, anchored by Virgil van Dijk, represents a step up from anything Japan have faced in recent friendlies, making that price worth taking.
Netherlands arrive in Dallas carrying significant injury concerns but still possessing one of the deepest squads at the tournament. Ronald Koeman’s side went unbeaten through UEFA qualifying, scoring 27 goals and conceding just four across eight matches. The core of their 2022 squad, Van Dijk, Frenkie de Jong, Memphis Depay, and Denzel Dumfries, returns with added Premier League muscle from Ryan Gravenberch, Cody Gakpo, and Tijjani Reijnders. The question is not quality but fitness: Depay arrives carrying a hamstring problem, and the absences of Xavi Simons and Jerdy Schouten through ACL injuries have thinned the midfield options.
Advertisement
Japan under Hajime Moriyasu have developed into a genuinely feared opponent. Their 3-4-2-1 base structure, aggressive pressing, and rapid counter-attacking have delivered scalps against elite opponents across the past four years. The loss of Kaoru Mitoma to a hamstring injury shifts the creative responsibility firmly onto Takefusa Kubo and Ritsu Doan, but with Ayase Ueda leading the line after a 25-goal Eredivisie season at Feyenoord, Japan have a clinical edge up front. The Netherlands vs Japan betting odds reflect a close contest, with the gap between the sides smaller than in previous editions of this fixture.
Where this game is won or lost is in the transition. Netherlands will want to control possession and funnel play through De Jong and Gravenberch; Japan will be content to press high, win the ball, and release Kubo and Ueda on the break. Koeman’s side handled that challenge adequately against Poland in qualifying but showed vulnerability in the 1-1 draws away from home. A tight, low-scoring match is the most probable outcome, though Japan’s recent form suggests an under-2.5 read may not be straightforward.
Recent Form & Trends
Netherlands last five results:
Ecuador (H): Drew 1-1 (Friendly)
Norway (H): Won 2-1 (Friendly)
Lithuania (H): Won 4-0 (World Cup Qualifying)
Poland (A): Drew 1-1 (World Cup Qualifying)
Finland (H): Won 4-0 (World Cup Qualifying)
Advertisement
Netherlands were relentless against the group’s weaker sides, hammering Lithuania 4-0 twice and putting four past Finland at home. The two draws came against Poland, the most competitive opponent in their qualifying pool, underlining that Koeman’s side can be contained by organized, physical opposition. Their friendly preparation offered mixed signals, a routine win over Norway followed by a draw with Ecuador pointing to a squad still finding its sharpest form heading into the tournament.
Japan last five results:
England (A): Won 1-0 (Friendly)
Bolivia (H): Won 3-0 (Kirin Cup)
Ghana (H): Won 2-0 (Kirin Cup)
Brazil (H): Won 3-2 (Kirin Cup)
Japan arrive on a five-match winning run, with the headline result being a 1-0 victory over England at Wembley in March 2026. The win over Brazil in October, sealed by a goal from Ueda, demonstrated that Japan’s high-press system can unsettle top-10 nations. Consecutive clean sheets against Scotland and England in the pre-tournament window suggest Moriyasu’s defensive shape is well-drilled, though the step up to a World Cup knockout environment against a Dutch side of this quality is still a genuine test.
Netherlands vs Japan History & H2H Trends
Netherlands and Japan have met three times, with the Dutch holding the upper hand across that limited sample. The most significant encounter came at the 2010 FIFA World Cup in South Africa, where Netherlands edged Japan 1-0 in the group stage on their way to the final. A 2013 friendly ended 2-2, while a 2009 friendly saw Netherlands win 3-0. The Netherlands have never lost to Japan in three meetings, though the 2013 draw showed Japan are capable of matching them on the day. No meeting in over a decade means the historical record offers only limited guidance for Netherlands vs Japan prediction purposes.
Advertisement
Injuries, Suspensions & Roster News
Netherlands are dealing with a significant list of absentees and fitness doubts. Xavi Simons and Jerdy Schouten are both ruled out with ACL injuries suffered in April, which strips Koeman of two key midfield options. Matthijs de Ligt has not fully recovered from a back problem. Memphis Depay arrives having sustained a serious hamstring injury late in his club season at Corinthians. Tijjani Reijnders and Nathan Aké had restricted minutes toward the end of their club campaigns. Denzel Dumfries missed four months of club football but has returned. The depth of the squad means these losses are absorbable, though not trivial, and Koeman may be forced into tactical compromise in the engine room.
Japan are without Kaoru Mitoma, who suffered a hamstring injury following his goal in the win over England and will miss the tournament entirely. Wataru Endo, only recently back from foot surgery, carries a fitness question that matters given his centrality to Japan’s midfield structure. Takehiro Tomiyasu, another key player, may be limited to a bench role early in the tournament. The absence of Mitoma in particular is significant: it was his goal at Wembley that announced Japan’s readiness for this tournament, and the creative load now falls almost entirely on Kubo and Doan from wide areas.
Expected Lineups
Netherlands (4-3-3): Verbruggen; Dumfries, Van de Ven, Van Dijk (c), Aké; Gravenberch, De Jong, Reijnders; Malen, Depay, Gakpo
Advertisement
Japan (3-4-2-1): Suzuki; Itakura, Ito, Tomiyasu; Sugawara, Endo, Kamada, Seko; Kubo, Doan; Ueda
Predicted XI: squads to be confirmed.
Key Matchup to Watch
The contest between Takefusa Kubo and the Netherlands left side will shape the game. Koeman’s preferred 4-3-3 places significant defensive responsibility on the left back and the nearest midfielder to cover wide threats, and with Kubo drifting in from the right flank and Ritsu Doan cutting in from the opposite channel, Japan’s transition from their 3-4-2-1 into attack will repeatedly target that corridor. Nathan Aké, on the left of the Dutch back four, has shown vulnerability to quick, technical wide forwards during his club season. Kubo has 7 international goals in 49 caps and registered 3 goals in Japan’s recent form run, and if he can draw defenders and play Ueda into space, Japan’s best route to a goal runs directly through that channel.

The Netherlands vs Japan best bets centre on a tight, well-organized contest with the Dutch holding a structural edge but Japan fully capable of making it uncomfortable.
Main Pick: Netherlands to Win @ +112 (BetOnline) – Koeman’s side went 6W 2D 0L in qualifying, scoring 27 and conceding 4, and possess a defensive unit anchored by Van Dijk that Japan will struggle to breach consistently. The price is fair and the Dutch edge in individual quality at the back makes this the value call in a closely contested match.
Goals Market: Under 2.5 Goals @ -110 (BetOnline) – Netherlands conceded just 4 goals in 8 qualifying matches and Japan kept clean sheets in their last two friendlies against Scotland and England. Both managers prioritize defensive solidity over attacking adventure in high-stakes openers. The totals line of 2.5 at -110 reflects a balanced market, but the underlying defensive data points under.
Scorer Market: Cody Gakpo to Score Anytime – Gakpo is Netherlands’ most dynamic attacking option from the left and has the movement and finishing to punish a Japanese defensive line that will have to manage multiple threats. He is the go-to creative outlet when Depay’s fitness is uncertain.
Netherlands vs Japan Score Prediction: 1-0 Netherlands – A single-goal Dutch win is consistent with the head-to-head record, both teams’ defensive strength, and the expected game management approach from Koeman in a tournament opener.
Betting Odds & Lines
Current Netherlands vs Japan betting odds across approved operators for the Group F opener on June 14:
Best available price on a Netherlands win is +112 at BetOnline. The draw is available at +265 at best, and Japan’s best price to take all three points is +275 at Lucky Rebel or BetNow.

How to Watch & Where to Bet
How to Watch
Netherlands vs Japan kicks off at 4:00 PM ET on Sunday, June 14, 2026 at AT&T Stadium in Dallas (Arlington). US viewers can watch on Fox Sports and Telemundo. International viewers can access the match through Fox and Telemundo in the US, ITV and BBC in the UK, NOS in the Netherlands, RTE and Virgin Media in Ireland, and beIN Sports and TF1 in France.
How to Bet
To place a wager on this Group F opener, follow these steps:
Choose one of the approved operators: BetOnline, Lucky Rebel, or BetNow.
Navigate to the operator’s official site and create an account if you do not already have one.
Complete the identity verification process as required.
Make a deposit using your preferred payment method, including crypto options at BetNow.
Navigate to the Soccer or World Cup 2026 section of the sportsbook.
Locate Netherlands vs Japan under Group F fixtures for June 14.
Select your market, match result, totals, or player props, and enter your stake.
Review your betslip and confirm your wager before the June 14 kickoff.
Responsible Gambling
Gambling should be approached as entertainment, not a source of income. If betting is causing concern for you or someone close to you, contact the National Council on Problem Gambling helpline at 1-800-522-4700 or visit ncpgambling.org. Support is also available through Gamblers Anonymous. Set limits before you play and gamble within your means.
